Record defects and notify supervision immediately. , Clean and sanitize food-contact surfaces., Weekly-Lubricate moving parts with approved food-grade lubricant; check belts, seals, and alignment, , Monthly-Inspect seals, gaskets, and wear points more closely., Belts, bearings, fasteners, alignment, guards, Verify no buildup of dust, product residue, or debris. , Checking Belts, pneumatics, fill check, Inspect all critical components more thoroughly. , Tighten loose nuts, bolts, and hardware. , Quarterly-Replace worn belts, seals, gaskets, washers, and similar items as needed. Inspect bearings, gears, motors, wear parts and pulleys for wear. , Replace worn parts as needed. , Verify machine calibration and operating condition. , Oil level, gearbox, drive train, motor condition, Disassemble as needed or where required, deep clean, calibrate, lubricate, reassemble, verify safety devices, and test operation. Remove covers or panels as needed for deeper inspection., Shell/drum wear, liner condition, discharge condition, Verify sanitation and readiness for production., Full drive inspection, belt tension, hardware torque, Major inspection/overhaul; replace high-wear components based on condition and review maintenance history., Annually-Review repeated issues and update the PM schedule., Reassess spare parts inventory and lubrication program.
